staleProjectReview.note: Warning-only stale/archive review: visitor-facing rows are listed by repo; suppressed catalog rows are summarized by public reason code without exposing suppressed identifiers.

executableDownloadTrustShortlist.note: Filename-derived prioritization only; binaries are not downloaded or cryptographically verified. nextAction names the highest-impact missing evidence (checksums before attestation before SBOM).

releaseAssetDrift.note: Release asset filename inspection compares catalog downloadKind labels against uploaded latest-release asset names; source-only releases remain repo actions.

userscriptInstallTrust.note: Userscript metadata inspection parses raw .user.js headers only; script bodies are not executed.

catalogFeedAccounting.note: Public-safe accounting confirms each catalog row is exported as a project, exported as a redacted suppression, or flagged without exposing omitted repo names.

portfolioCompatibility.note: Compatibility snapshot for the downstream portfolio feed importer; normal consumers should use payload.projects and ignore unknown additive fields.

dependabotSecurityPosture.evidence: Dependabot security updates are enabled, and local version-update config is present for 3 ecosystem(s).
dependabotSecurityPosture.nextAction: Keep monitoring Dependabot alert volume and grouped version-update PRs.

codeScanning.reason: Detected repository languages do not include a current CodeQL-supported source language.

rows.localEvidence: Required-check readiness and direct-main maintenance policy are tracked separately; enabling branch-protection enforcement is a repository policy decision, not an unclassified local code gap.
rows.nextAction: Keep required-check readiness evidence current and enable enforcement only after the direct-main maintenance policy changes.

rows.localEvidence: OpenSSF Best Practices badge enrollment is an external manual governance program, not a local repository defect.
rows.nextAction: Enroll in the OpenSSF Best Practices program only if the maintainer wants the external badge workflow.

rows.localEvidence: PR delivery and required checks are proven; pull request review enforcement remains warning-only until an independent reviewer or team model exists.
rows.nextAction: Define an independent reviewer or team model before requiring pull request reviews; keep required-check PR delivery in the meantime.

rows.localEvidence: This repository is a deterministic profile README/catalog generator with Pester fixtures rather than a binary parser or network service.
rows.nextAction: Consider property-based generator tests if catalog input handling becomes broader or riskier.

rows.localEvidence: The live language mix is PowerShell-only; CodeQL is not applicable, while PSScriptAnalyzer, actionlint, zizmor, and Scorecard SARIF run locally or in CI.
rows.nextAction: Reopen the CodeQL posture decision when a CodeQL-supported language appears.

scorecardAlertPosture.note: Rows classify open Scorecard SARIF alerts as local fixes, hosted-refresh waits, accepted tool limitations, or external governance items; the posture remains warning-only.

generatedPrCredentialDecision.rationale: The manual generated-PR job already scopes write privileges to actions:write, contents:write, and pull-requests:write; enabling the repository setting avoids adding a long-lived automation secret.
generatedPrCredentialDecision.nextAction: Use the proven generated-profile/validation PR status handoff as the generated-maintenance path, then document the direct-main maintenance bypass or PR-delivery policy before required-check enforcement.

generatedPrDryRunEvidence.evidenceSummary: Manual hosted dry-run completed Regenerate profile, summary, artifact upload, and Preview pull request after workflow artifact-runtime and summary-size guard updates. The helper planned automation/profile-sync-27084524165 without creating a branch, commit, push, pull request, or validation dispatch.
generatedPrDryRunEvidence.nextAction: Keep refreshing this evidence after dry-run workflow changes; continue resolving live PR delivery and required-check preconditions before enforcement.

generatedPrWriteEvidence.pullRequestCheckRollupNote: PR #10 statusCheckRollup contained StatusContext generated-profile/validation with state SUCCESS and target https://github.com/SysAdminDoc/SysAdminDoc/actions/runs/27087806797.
generatedPrWriteEvidence.blocker: Required-check enforcement still needs a documented direct-main maintenance bypass or a broader PR-delivery switch before admin-enforced required checks can be enabled.
generatedPrWriteEvidence.evidenceSummary: Hosted write-pr drill 27087776182 ran on c3aeeb2, continued past the workflow-permissions endpoint 403, committed 7e1ea63 to automation/profile-sync-27087776182, pushed the branch, published generated-profile/validation as pending, created PR #10, and dispatched Profile sync validation run 27087806797. Validation used sync-profile.ps1 -Write -Check, passed, uploaded profile-sync-report artifact 7462523830 and rendered-profile-smoke artifact 7462524019, then the generated-validation-status job updated generated-profile/validation to success. PR #10 statusCheckRollup and gh pr checks both reported generated-profile/validation as passing, then PR #10 was closed and the generated branch was deleted after evidence collection.
generatedPrWriteEvidence.nextAction: Document or implement the direct-main maintenance bypass/PR-delivery policy needed before enabling admin-enforced required-check protection.

directMainMaintenancePolicy.evidence: No direct-main bypass actor is approved. Routine maintenance uses pull-request delivery; PR #14 proved routine PR delivery before enforcement, and PR #16 proved it under active required checks.
directMainMaintenancePolicy.nextAction: Keep routine maintenance on pull-request delivery unless a separate approved bypass is documented.

candidateCheckExercisePlan.nonMutationPolicy: Open a disposable PR only for check creation proof, do not merge it, do not enable branch protection or rulesets, then close the PR and delete the proof branch after evidence collection.
verificationSteps: Create a temporary branch from current main with prefix automation/required-check-proof-.
Make a no-risk proof commit that touches README.md, .github/workflows/tests.yml, and setup.ps1 while preserving generated profile output.
Open a disposable pull request and wait for all candidate checks to appear in the PR check rollup.
Verify pull_request and merge_group-capable workflow check names match the candidate list.
Close the disposable pull request and delete the proof branch after recording run IDs and check conclusions.
candidateCheckExercisePlan.nextAction: Keep this proof fresh before enforcement; routine PR delivery is now proven by PR #14.

candidateCheckExerciseEvidence.evidenceSummary: Disposable PR #13 created all six candidate required-check names. Check generated README, PSScriptAnalyzer, Pester (offline), Markdownlint, Windows setup smoke, and zizmor passed. The PR was closed and automation/required-check-proof-20260607-125 was deleted after evidence collection.
candidateCheckExerciseEvidence.nextAction: Keep this proof fresh before enabling required-check enforcement; refresh it if GitHub's recent-check selection window expires.

routineMaintenancePrDrillEvidence.evidenceSummary: Routine maintenance PR #14 merged by rebase after all six candidate checks passed. GitHub deleted the routine-pr-drill-evidence branch after merge. Squash and merge-commit methods are disabled for this repository.
routineMaintenancePrDrillEvidence.nextAction: Required-check enforcement proof is now recorded by PR #16; keep future maintenance on PR delivery.

requiredCheckEnforcementEvidence.evidenceSummary: PR #16 was the first normal maintenance pull request after branch-protection required checks were enabled. GitHub required all six candidate checks, every candidate check passed on head SHA 8575e324182b96527bb9b58420d5ff44e3c05c06, and the pull request merged by rebase.
requiredCheckEnforcementEvidence.nextAction: Keep monitoring required checks on routine pull requests and re-query branch protection after check-name changes.

items.summary: Candidate required-check names are defined and must stay stable.
items.nextAction: Keep required-check job names unique and unchanged before enabling enforcement.

items.summary: Candidate workflows must create checks for pull requests and merge queue runs.
items.nextAction: Fix missing pull_request or merge_group triggers before making any check required.

items.summary: Each required check must have a recent successful run in this repository before it can be selected.
items.evidence: Disposable PR #13 created all six candidate checks and every candidate check completed successfully.
items.nextAction: Refresh this disposable PR proof if GitHub's recent-check selection window expires before enforcement.

items.summary: Direct-main delivery must be replaced by PR delivery or a documented bypass before enforcement.
items.evidence: Routine maintenance PR #14 merged by rebase after Check generated README, PSScriptAnalyzer, Pester (offline), Markdownlint, Windows setup smoke, and zizmor all passed. The proof branch was deleted after merge.
items.nextAction: Select and enable one required-check enforcement mechanism, then re-query branch protection or rulesets.

items.summary: Choose branch protection or a repository ruleset only after PR delivery is ready.
items.evidence: Live branch-protection and ruleset state must be validated before selecting an enforcement mechanism.
items.nextAction: After PR delivery is proven, enable one enforcement mechanism and re-query branch protection/rulesets.
